propositions: Build a benchmark of five mechanically-seeded defect classes (absent asserted capability, layer-cake split, consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge, unnecessary mechanism, untagged operational AC) from real cleanly-closed plans, where the mutation itself is the label.

Select base plans via a codebase-grounded cleanly-closed filter (post_claim_edit_class none, no reopens, no force_close, no verifier fails, persisted review, live closed status, and ≥2 children for container classes 2 and 3).

Run the CURRENT plan-review gate over each mutant and control to record a baseline detection/FP table, which is a reference point (expected near-zero for miss classes) rather than E2's own pass condition.

Execute the production gate via a verified separable-root mechanism that redirects ticket reads to a tmp mutant store while grounding code against the real repo, using review_plan with sign/emit_sidecar off and force on.
Isolate the run by constructing the mutant store as a standalone git clone with its own .git directory so mutation commits cannot reach the real tickets branch, asserted before any injection.

Enforce counting and run-validity rules as golden-tested pure helpers, treating INDETERMINATE verdicts as non-detections/non-FPs and refusing to emit a baseline when any class exceeds the 0.20 indeterminate cap.
